title: Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil

seo.title: Focaccia Genovese: Authentic Rosemary Olive Oil Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Focaccia Genovese with rosemary and olive oil. This easy recipe brings the taste of Liguria to your kitchen. Perfect for sharing!
longTailKeywords: Focaccia Genovese rosemary olive oil recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Focaccia Genovese with Rosemary and Olive Oil
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Focaccia Genovese with rosemary and olive oil, golden brown and delicious
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a slice of traditional Focaccia Genovese, a Ligurian classic infused with fragrant rosemary and rich olive oil.

description: Savor the true taste of Liguria with this authentic Focaccia Genovese. Infused with aromatic rosemary and rich olive oil, its soft, airy crumb and crispy crust make it an irresistible Italian classic. Perfect as a side, snack, or appetizer, bringing a piece of Genoa to your table.

instructions.title: Activate Yeast
instructions.text: In a large bowl, dissolve the fresh yeast (or active dry yeast) in the lukewarm water. Let it sit for 5 minutes until foamy, indicating the yeast is active.

instructions.title: Mix Dough
instructions.text: Add the flour and fine sea salt to the yeast mixture. Mix with a spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms. Gradually add 30ml of the extra virgin olive oil, kneading gently for about 5-7 minutes until the dough comes together and is slightly sticky.

instructions.title: First Rise (Bulk Fermentation)
instructions.text: Transfer the dough to a lightly oiled bowl, turning once to coat. Cover with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let it rise in a warm place for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until doubled in size.

instructions.title: Shape and Dimple
instructions.text: Gently transfer the risen dough to a well-oiled 30x40cm (12x16 inch) baking tray. Using your fingertips, gently stretch and dimple the dough to fill the entire tray. Do not press too hard.

instructions.title: Second Rise (Proofing)
instructions.text: Drizzle the remaining 20ml of extra virgin olive oil over the dimpled focaccia. Sprinkle with chopped fresh rosemary and coarse sea salt. Cover loosely with plastic wrap and let it rise again for 30-45 minutes in a warm place. Preheat your oven to 220°C (425°F) during this time.

instructions.title: Bake Focaccia
instructions.text: Bake the focaccia in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown and cooked through. The crust should be crisp and the interior soft and airy.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Once baked, remove the focaccia from the oven. For an extra rich flavor, you can drizzle a little more extra virgin olive oil over the warm focaccia. Let it cool on a wire rack for a few minutes before slicing and serving. Buon appetito!

tips: For an exceptionally airy crumb, ensure your dough is well-hydrated. The dough should be sticky but manageable; resist the urge to add too much extra flour.
Use high-quality extra virgin olive oil, especially for the final drizzle. It significantly enhances the authentic flavor and aroma of the Focaccia Genovese.
Proper dimpling is key for the characteristic texture. Use your fingertips to create deep indentations without tearing the dough, allowing oil to pool and create those delicious crispy pockets.
article:
**Unlocking the Secrets of Focaccia Genovese: A Rosemary Olive Oil Masterpiece**

Imagine a golden-crusted flatbread, incredibly soft and airy on the inside, with a slight chewiness that gives way to an explosion of savory flavor. Each bite is infused with the aromatic essence of fresh rosemary and the rich, fruity notes of high-quality extra virgin olive oil. This isn't just any bread; this is Focaccia Genovese, a true jewel of Italian baking, hailing from the sun-drenched region of Liguria. More than a simple accompaniment, it's a culinary experience that embodies the heart and soul of Mediterranean cuisine. If you’ve ever dreamt of recreating that authentic taste of Italy in your own kitchen, perfecting the art of Focaccia Genovese is a delightful journey.

## What is Focaccia Genovese?

Focaccia Genovese is the quintessential focaccia, often considered the "original" and most traditional version of this beloved Italian flatbread. Distinguishing itself from other focaccias, the Genovese style is characterized by its signature dimpled surface, which acts as tiny wells to hold a generous pooling of olive oil and a delicate brine or emulsion. This unique technique is crucial for achieving its characteristic crispy, golden crust and incredibly tender, airy crumb. Unlike some denser bread, Focaccia Genovese is light yet substantial, with a subtly salty flavor profile beautifully complemented by fresh rosemary and the robust taste of premium olive oil. It’s a versatile bread, perfect on its own, as an appetizer, or as a delightful base for sandwiches.

## The History of Focaccia Genovese

The story of focaccia is as old as bread itself, with roots tracing back to ancient times when flatbreads were cooked over hearths (Latin: *focus*). However, the specific iteration known as Focaccia Genovese, or *fügassa* in the local Ligurian dialect, firmly originates from Genoa, the capital of Liguria, a crescent-shaped coastal region in northwest Italy. For centuries, this simple yet profoundly satisfying bread has been a staple in Ligurian homes and bakeries. Traditionally enjoyed for breakfast, often dipped in cappuccino or coffee, it has also long served as a convenient snack for sailors departing from Genoa's bustling port. Its evolution from a humble baker's treat to a globally recognized delicacy speaks to its enduring appeal and perfect balance of simple, high-quality ingredients. The emphasis on olive oil, a cornerstone of Ligurian agriculture, has always been central to its identity, making it a true reflection of its culinary heritage.

## Ingredients for Focaccia Genovese

Creating authentic Focaccia Genovese relies on a few key, high-quality ingredients. Each plays a vital role in achieving its signature texture and flavor:

*   **Strong Bread Flour:** A high-protein flour (around 12-14%) is essential for developing sufficient gluten, which gives the focaccia its characteristic chewiness and open, airy crumb.
*   **Water:** Lukewarm water helps activate the yeast effectively.
*   **Active Dry Yeast (or Instant Yeast):** The leavening agent responsible for the bread's rise.
*   **Fine Sea Salt:** Crucial for flavor and also controls yeast activity.
*   **Extra Virgin Olive Oil:** This is non-negotiable. Use the best quality you can find, preferably from Liguria or a similarly fruity, robust variety. It's used in the dough, for oiling the pan, and generously on top.
*   **Fresh Rosemary:** Essential for the classic aroma and flavor. Dried can be used in a pinch, but fresh is vastly superior.
*   **Flaky Sea Salt:** For sprinkling on top, providing bursts of briny crunch.
*   **Brine/Emulsion:** A simple mixture of water, olive oil, and a pinch of salt, poured over the dimpled dough before baking. This is the secret to the crispy crust and moist interior.

## Step-by-Step Focaccia Genovese Recipe

This recipe yields one large focaccia, perfect for sharing.

**Equipment:** Large mixing bowl, stand mixer (optional, but highly recommended), 9x13 inch baking pan, plastic wrap, clean kitchen towel.

**Ingredients:**

*   500g strong bread flour
*   400ml lukewarm water (around 100-110°F / 38-43°C)
*   7g active dry yeast (2 ¼ teaspoons)
*   10g fine sea salt (1 ½ teaspoons)
*   60ml extra virgin olive oil, plus more for the pan and topping
*   2 sprigs fresh rosemary, leaves picked
*   1 tablespoon flaky sea salt, for topping

**For the Brine/Emulsion:**

*   3 tablespoons water
*   3 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil
*   ¼ teaspoon fine sea salt

**Instructions:**

1.  **Activate Yeast:** In a large bowl (or stand mixer bowl), combine the lukewarm water and yeast. Let sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy.
2.  **Make the Dough:** Add the flour, fine sea salt, and 60ml olive oil to the yeast mixture. If using a stand mixer, attach the dough hook and knead on low speed for 8-10 minutes until the dough is smooth, elastic, and pulls away from the sides of the bowl. If kneading by hand, mix until combined, then turn out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 10-15 minutes until smooth. The dough will be quite wet and sticky.
3.  **First Rise (Bulk Fermentation):** Transfer the dough to a generously olive-oiled large bowl, turning to coat. Cover tightly with plastic wrap or a damp kitchen towel. Let rise in a warm place for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until doubled in size.
4.  **Prepare Pan & Dimple:** Generously coat a 9x13 inch baking pan with olive oil (don't be shy!). Gently deflate the risen dough and carefully transfer it to the prepared pan. Using your oiled fingertips, gently press and stretch the dough to fill the pan, being careful not to tear it.
5.  **Second Rise (Proofing):** Once the dough roughly fills the pan, generously drizzle more olive oil over the surface. Using your fingertips, create deep dimples all over the dough. This is crucial for the classic Focaccia Genovese texture. Sprinkle with fresh rosemary leaves. Cover lightly with plastic wrap and let rise again for 30-45 minutes, or until visibly puffy.
6.  **Preheat Oven:** While the dough is proofing, preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C).
7.  **Add Brine & Bake:** Whisk together the brine ingredients (water, olive oil, salt). Pour this mixture evenly over the dimpled focaccia. Sprinkle generously with flaky sea salt.
8.  **Bake:** Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until deep golden brown on top and bottom.
9.  **Cool:** Once baked, immediately transfer the Focaccia Genovese to a wire rack to cool slightly. This prevents the bottom from becoming soggy. Serve warm.

## Tips for Perfect Focaccia Genovese

Achieving that perfect, airy, and flavorful Focaccia Genovese isn't difficult, but a few key tips can elevate your results:

*   **Quality Olive Oil is King:** This cannot be stressed enough. Since olive oil is such a dominant flavor, investing in a good quality extra virgin olive oil will make an enormous difference.
*   **High Hydration Dough:** Don't be afraid of the sticky dough. A high hydration level (more water relative to flour) is what contributes to the airy, open crumb. Resist the urge to add too much extra flour during kneading.
*   **Proper Kneading:** Develop the gluten sufficiently. This is easier with a stand mixer, but achievable by hand with patience. Well-developed gluten leads to a better texture.
*   **Deep Dimples:** Don't be shy when dimpling the dough. These depressions are where the olive oil and brine collect, creating those coveted crispy, flavorful pockets.
*   **Don't Skimp on the Brine:** The water-oil-salt emulsion is the secret weapon. It penetrates the dough, keeping it moist while the top crisps up.
*   **Bake Hot:** A hot oven helps create a beautiful crust and a quick, airy rise.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings

Focaccia Genovese is incredibly versatile. It's often enjoyed simply on its own, perhaps with a glass of local Ligurian wine like Vermentino or Pigato.

*   **As an Appetizer:** Serve it with a platter of cured meats, cheeses, and olives.
*   **Dipping:** It's excellent for dipping into more olive oil, balsamic vinegar, or a rich tomato sauce.
*   **With Meals:** A perfect accompaniment to soups, stews, or grilled meats and fish.
*   **Sandwiches:** Slice it horizontally to make delicious sandwiches with fresh mozzarella, tomatoes, pesto, or your favorite deli meats.
*   **Breakfast:** In Genoa, it's traditionally enjoyed for breakfast, often dipped in coffee or milk!

## Storage and Reheating

Focaccia Genovese is best enjoyed fresh, ideally on the day it's baked. However, if you have leftovers:

*   **Storage:** Store in an airtight container or wrapped tightly in foil at room temperature for up to 1-2 days. It will lose some of its crispness but will still be delicious.
*   **Freezing:** For longer storage, slice the cooled focaccia and freeze in a single layer before transferring to a freezer-safe bag or container. It can be frozen for up to 2-3 months.
*   **Reheating:** To revive its texture, reheat slices in a preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) for 5-10 minutes, or in a toaster oven until warmed through and slightly crispy. Avoid microwaving, as it can make the focaccia chewy.

## Variations to Try

While the classic Focaccia Genovese with rosemary and olive oil is perfect as is, you can certainly experiment with other toppings:

*   **Olives:** Add pitted Kalamata or Ligurian Taggiasca olives before the second rise.
*   **Cherry Tomatoes:** Halved cherry tomatoes pressed into the dough add sweetness and moisture.
*   **Onions:** Thinly sliced red onions, caramelized or raw, are a delightful addition.
*   **Herbs:** While rosemary is traditional, try a mix of thyme and oregano for a different aromatic profile.
*   **Cheese:** A sprinkling of grated Parmesan or Pecorino Romano during the last few minutes of baking can add a salty, cheesy crust.

Embracing the simplicity and quality of ingredients is key to mastering this exceptional Italian flatbread. Give this Focaccia Genovese recipe a try, and transport your taste buds straight to the sun-drenched Ligurian coast!
